source: Majidi, A.G., Ramos, V., Rosa-Santos, P., das Neves, L., Taveira-Pinto, F. (2025). Power production assessment of wave energy converters in mainland Portugal. Renewable Energy 243, 122540.
note: Power matrices digitised from the paper's appendix. For each device, period_s is the wave-period axis (period_type: Te/Tp/Tz, seconds) and hs_m is significant wave height Hm0 (metres). power_kW[i][j] is electrical power in kW at period_s[i] and hs_m[j]. Zeros outside the operating envelope are the device's own cut-in/cut-out.
hs_note: All matrices are indexed on Hm0 (significant wave height). SWAN's significant wave height output (HSIGN / 'Hs') is the spectral Hm0 = 4*sqrt(m0), so it maps directly onto the Hm0 axis with no conversion.
period_note: Each device's power matrix uses one of three wave periods (period_type). To run a device, look it up on the matching SWAN variable given in period_swan (Te->Tm-1,0, Tp->Tp, Tz->Tm02). SWAN provides Hm0, Tp, Tm-1,0 and Tm02 in the CI domain and the point time series, so every device can use its exact index. Note the Galway Bay seasonal-MEAN maps store only Hs, Tm-1,0, Dir and P (no Tp), so Tp-indexed devices need the raw/CI data, not those pre-averaged GB maps.
